shortcomings: No explicit front-end or web UI development experience evident (no HTML/CSS/JavaScript, React/Angular/Vue, or responsive UI work shown in Skills or Projects).
Projects and work history are heavily ML/backend/API/API-architecture focused; lack of customer-facing web application deliverables to demonstrate frontend contribution.
No dedicated summary/profile section to align with a web developer role or to call out remote/contractor suitability.
Education line shows a Master of Science with May 2026 as a fixed date rather than an expected date (could confuse ATS/hiring managers).
Frontend collaboration with design teams is not clearly evidenced (only generic “cross-functional teams” mention); no explicit design-to-dev workflow or UI/UX collaboration examples.

Portfolio/website evidence exists, but the resume does not explicitly connect frontend work or show web development outcomes (e.g., dashboards, web apps) beyond ML dashboards and observability tooling.

Limited emphasis on maintainable code/documentation practices from a web development perspective (described as ML/arch for ML systems; not explicit about frontend code quality, testing, or documentation standards).
Remote-working capabilities and distributed-team experience are not clearly described (role location is India; remote work is stated in job brief but not demonstrated in resume).
missing_keywords: Portfolio links or explicit frontend projects/examples
experience_gaps: No demonstrated frontend/web UI development experience to satisfy a Web Developer role.
No explicit mention of building customer-facing web apps or dashboards with frontend tech stacks.
Backend/API integration is present (API Gateway, Lambda) but no evidence of frontend data binding or UI integration workflows.
Absence of front-end project deliverables to show impact on user experience (UI/UX metrics, user studies, or design-to-dev handoffs).
Remote-work experience is not clearly documented (no explicit distributed-team collaboration examples).
weak_sections: Education section uses a fixed May 2026 date for Master’s degree (preferable to mark as Expected May 2026).
Technical Skills are heavily ML/AI-centric; no dedicated frontend stack (HTML/CSS/JS, React) listed.
Project Experience is ML-centric with strong RAG/NLP focus; lacks web development projects or outcomes that demonstrate building responsive user interfaces.
Work Experience bullets focus on backend/cloud architecture and ML tooling; not explicit evidence of shipping web-facing features to end users or clients.
No summary/profile section to quickly align candidate’s strengths with the Web Developer role; resume reads as ML/AI engineer-focused rather than web developer.
overall_assessment: The candidate demonstrates strong backend, ML/AI, and API-driven engineering experience with solid accomplishments and measurable impact. However, there is a significant mismatch with the Web Developer (frontend) requirements of the role: explicit HTML/CSS/JavaScript proficiency, frontend frameworks, and demonstrable web UI deliverables are not present. The resume would benefit from a dedicated frontend-focused section or projects (e.g., dashboards, responsive websites, or user-facing UI) and explicit references to collaboration with design teams, front-end testing, accessibility, and maintainable frontend code practices. Additionally, rephrasing the education date as Expected May 2026 and adding a concise summary to align with the contractor/web dev opportunity would improve ATS compatibility and immediate relevance. If the candidate cannot show frontend experience, consider tailoring to a hybrid role emphasizing API integration and backend support for frontend teams, or target ML-focused web tooling (admin dashboards, data visualization UI) with concrete frontend deliverables.
